student_answer: Looking at the mapped SMIRKS, I need to trace the atom-mapped centers through the transformation. **Reactant side:** - N:21 is N⁺ with H:20, double-bonded to a ring carbon (cyclohexadienone), single-bonded to pyrrolidine - N:10 is NH₂ on a pyrrolidine ring (hydrazine-type) - Ring has C=O (ketone) **Product side:** - N:21 is neutral N, double-bonded to ring carbon (now imine C=N:21), still bonded to pyrrolidine — lost H:20 and +1 charge - N:10 is NH₂⁺ bonded to pyrrolidine — gained H:20 and +1 charge - Ring C=O is gone; O leaves as water - C=N:21 imine formed **Reaction center summary:** Bonds broken: N:21–H:20, C–O (ring ketone) Bonds formed: N:10–H:20, C=N:21 (ring imine) Charge changes: N:21 (+1→0), N:10 (0→+1) final_answer("Bonds broken: N:21-H:20, C-O(ring ketone); Bonds formed: N:10-H:20, C=N:21(ring imine); Charge changes: N:21(+1→0), N:10(0→+1)")

student_answer: Looking at the mapped SMIRKS transformation: **Reactant:** C1C[C:20]([OH:10])(O)[OH+:21]C1 — cyclobutane with geminal [OH:10]/[OH+:21] on [C:20] **Product:** C(C[C:20](=[OH+:10])O)C[OH+:21] — open-chain with carbonyl at [C:20] and [OH+:21] on terminal C Reaction center bond edits: - **Broken:** C–C ring bond (between [C:20] and adjacent ring carbon), C:20–OH+:21 single bond - **Formed:** C(ring terminal)–OH+:21 single bond, C:20=OH+:10 double bond - **Order change:** C:20–OH:10 single → double This is a ring-opening fragmentation of a protonated gem-diol cyclobutane, yielding an open-chain aldehyde/ketone with hydroxyl migration. final_answer("Bonds broken: C-C(ring), C:20-OH+:21; Bonds formed: C-OH+:21(terminal), C:20=OH+:10; Bond order change: C:20-OH:10 single→double")

New C–C bond: C:10–C:20 2. π-bond cleavage: C:10=C:11 and C:20=C:21 3. π-bond formation: C:11=N⁺:12 and C:21=C:22 4. Charge transfer: N⁺:23 → N:23; N:12 → N⁺:12 No ring is formed — this is an open-chain addition of a 1,3-dipole to a dipolarophile, not a [3+2] cycloaddition. The azomethine ylide adds across the Michael acceptor in a stepwise conjugate addition fashion. **Mechanistic class:** 1,3-dipolar addition **Reaction center:** C:10=C:11–N:12 / C:20=C:21–C:22=N⁺:23 1,3-dipolar addition; reaction_center: {C:10, C:11, N:12, C:20, C:21, C:22, N:23}

    "procedure_text": "Example 6\nStep 1: Preparation of methyl 2-chloro-6-(4-cyanophenoxy) nicotinate\nA 250 mL eggplant flask was sequentially charged with methyl 2,6-dichloronicotinate (2.06 g, 10 mmol), 4-hydroxybenzonitrile (1.20 g, 10 mmol), and 12 mL of N, N-dimethylformamide for dissolving them. Triethylamine (1.80 ml, 13 mmol) was added dropwise under stirring at room temperature, and after completion of the dropwise addition, triethylene diamine (168 mg, 1.50 mmol) was added. The mixture was stirred at room temperature for 4-5 hours and the solution changed from clear to turbid. Thin layer chromatography [V (petroleum ether) / V (ethyl acetate) = 6/1] detected that most of the raw material disappeared. Then, 1.30 mL of HOAc, 25 mL of isopropanol and 15 mL of ice water were sequentially added while the solution changed from turbid to clear, and stirred at room temperature for 0.5 hour. 40mL of water was slowly dropwise added, and after completion of the dropwise addition, stirred at room temperature for 2 hours. A large number of white solid precipitated and was filtered. The filter cake was washed with a mixed solution of isopropyl alcohol / water = 1: 1 and dried under vacuum at 50 °C for 8 hours to obtain 1.94 g of methyl 2-chloro-6-(4-cyanophenoxy) nicotinate as a solid, 67.36%."
